title: Create v3/about.html — About Offbeat, Inc.
body:
Create a new file v3/about.html that introduces Offbeat, Inc.

Requirements:
- Match the v3 dark futuristic design (same CSS variables, header, footer as index.html)
- Sections: company overview, what we build, tech stack, contact CTA
- Include JSON-LD Organization schema and standard OG/meta tags
- Add 'About' link to the nav on ALL existing v3 pages
- Do NOT use the words 'proprietary' or 'unconventional' in any copy
- Buttons must NOT lift/translate upward on hover
- Use the same Outfit+FiraCode fonts, dark bg #020206, glow cyan+violet accents

title: Create v3/work.html — DatBotty showcase of completed deployments
body:
Create a new file v3/work.html showcasing what DatBotty has autonomously built.

Requirements:
- Match v3 dark futuristic design (same header/footer as status.html)
- Grid of project cards showing: Offbeat website v3, SEO implementation, Cloud Agent pipeline, GitHub Actions automation
- Each card: title, short description, tech used as badge pills, live link where applicable
- Include JSON-LD ItemList schema
- Add 'Work' link to the nav on ALL existing v3 pages
- Buttons must NOT lift on hover

title: Mobile responsiveness audit: fix v3 layout issues on small screens
body:
Review all HTML files under v3/ for mobile layout problems at 375px and 768px widths.

Check:
- Navigation wrapping or overflowing
- Cards and grids too narrow or cramped
- Text rendering issues on mobile
- Any horizontal scroll triggers
- Footer alignment on small screens

Apply CSS-only fixes using @media queries. Do NOT change the desktop design or any content. Report all findings as a numbered list first, then apply fixes.

title: Add Recent Activity timeline section to v3/status.html
body:
Add a 'Recent Cloud Agent Activity' section to v3/status.html.

Requirements:
- A timeline/feed of the last 6-8 meaningful things DatBotty deployed
- Each item: icon + date + short description
- Example entries: v3/contact.html deployed, SEO structured data added to all pages, Cloud Agent pipeline established, 11 pages verified healthy
- Use the same dark v3 design with cyan accents
- Insert the section between the Workflows table and the footer
- Keep all existing content intact

title: Add 4 new entries to v3/faq.html
body:
Add 4 more FAQ entries to v3/faq.html. Add these questions:

1. What kind of tasks can DatBotty handle autonomously?
2. How does DatBotty avoid making harmful or irreversible changes?
3. Can DatBotty work while Crash is asleep?
4. What AI models does DatBotty use and do they cost money?

Keep the exact same accordion/FAQ styling. Add entries at the bottom of the existing list. Do NOT modify any existing entries or the page design.

title: Add BreadcrumbList schema to all v3 pages
Performance audit: add lazy loading and font preconnect to v3 pages
body:
Review all v3 HTML files for performance improvements:
- Add loading=lazy to any img tags missing it
- Add rel=preconnect for Google Fonts if not already present
- Check for any render-blocking resources in the head
- Verify viewport meta tag is present on all pages

Apply safe fixes only. Do NOT change design or content. Report findings first.

title: Add Cookie Policy and CCPA sections to v3/privacy.html
body:
Add two new sections to v3/privacy.html:

1. Cookie Policy — explain what cookies or localStorage the site uses (if any) and how to opt out
2. California Privacy Rights (CCPA) — standard notice for CA residents

Keep the same dark design, heading styles, and paragraph copy style as the rest of the page. Add the new sections at the bottom before the footer. Do NOT alter any existing sections.

title: Add Twitter Card meta tags to all v3 pages
body:
Add Twitter Card meta tags to all v3 HTML files that are missing them.

Required tags to add:
twitter:card set to summary
twitter:title set to match each page title
twitter:description set to match the existing og:description
twitter:site set to @offbeatinc

Check each file. Report which files were missing these. Apply to all v3 HTML files that need them.

title: Add pricing/tier section to v3/services.html
body:
Add an engagement tier section to v3/services.html.

Tiers to show:
- Discovery (free intro consultation)
- Project (fixed scope, custom quote)
- Ongoing (retainer, custom AI ops)

Three-column grid of tier cards matching the v3 dark futuristic design. Each card: tier name, short description, CTA linking to contact.html. Do NOT show dollar amounts — use Custom Quote or Contact Us.

Insert between the main services grid and the existing CTA section. Match existing CSS variables exactly. Buttons must NOT lift on hover.

title: Add Powered By tech stack section to v3/index.html
body:
Add a minimal Powered By section to v3/index.html listing the key technologies DatBotty runs on.

Tech to list: Python, GitHub Actions, GitHub Copilot Cloud Agent, OpenClaw, Gemini, SQLite, Docker, Playwright.

Compact row of pill/badge styled items using v3 dark design and cyan/violet palette. Insert between the feature cards section and the CTA at the bottom. Keep ALL existing content intact. Buttons must NOT lift on hover.

title: Accessibility: add skip-to-content link to all v3 pages
body:
Add a skip-to-content accessibility link at the top of every v3 HTML file.

Pattern: an anchor linking to #main-content with class skip-link, positioned off-screen and shown on focus.

CSS for the skip link: position absolute, left -9999px normally, position static on focus.

Also add id=main-content to the main element on each page.

Report which files were updated. Apply to all v3 HTML files.

title: Create v3/blog.html — Offbeat tech blog index page
body:
Create a new file v3/blog.html for an Offbeat, Inc. tech blog index.

Requirements:
- Match v3 dark design (dark background #020206, cyan/blue accents, same header/footer as index.html)
- Show 3 featured blog post cards in a grid layout:
  1. 'Building DatBotty: An AI System That Actually Works' — about the journey of building a 24/7 autonomous AI
  2. 'From Zero to 15 Pages: How AI Built Our Website While We Slept' — about GitHub Cloud Agent deployments
  3. 'The Tools Behind Offbeat: OpenClaw, GitHub Actions, and the Cloud Agent Pipeline' — tech stack overview
- Each card: title, date (April 2026), tag pill, short excerpt, 'Read More' link (href='#' placeholder)
- Page header: 'Thoughts from Offbeat'
- Include nav linking to v3/index.html, with Status link to status.html
- Add Blog link to nav on ALL existing v3 pages
- JSON-LD BlogPosting schema for the first post
- Buttons must NEVER lift or move on hover
- Do NOT use the words 'proprietary' or 'unconventional' in copy

title: Review and fix all canonical link tags in v3 pages
body:
Audit every HTML file under v3/ for canonical link tags.

Rules:
- Every page needs a canonical link pointing to its full URL at crashycrash.github.io/offbeat-website/v3/
- index.html canonical should point to the directory URL with trailing slash
- No page should be missing a canonical
- Make sure no page points to the wrong canonical

Check all files. Add or fix missing canonicals. Report which files needed changes.

title: Add animated stat counter to v3/index.html hero section
body:
Add a compact animated stat counter row to v3/index.html.

Stats to animate on scroll (count up from 0 using IntersectionObserver):
- 11+ Live Pages
- 24/7 Autonomous
- 93% Task Success
- 0x Core Model Cost

Insert the stats row directly below the hero headline and above the CTA buttons. Use a simple JS counter that animates on first scroll into view. Match v3 dark design with cyan gradient values. Do NOT alter any existing hero content.

title: Improve v3/contact.html with response time and FAQ callout
body:
Make two improvements to v3/contact.html:

1. Add a line near the contact form or CTA: Typical response time is 1-2 business days
2. Add a FAQ callout: Have a quick question? Check the FAQ first, with a link to faq.html

Keep all existing design, structure, and styling intact. Report what was changed.

title: Add Best Value badge to top product in v3/tools.html
body:
Review v3/tools.html:

1. Verify all affiliate links correctly use the offbeat0f-20 tag
2. Add a subtle Best Value badge overlay to ONE product card that most deserves highlighting
3. Badge style: small cyan pill in the top-right corner of the card, matching v3 CSS variables, border: 1px solid cyan, background with low opacity

Do NOT change any product descriptions, prices, or links beyond verifying affiliate tags. Report what was found and changed.

title: Add alt text and aria-label audit to all v3 images and icons
body:
Review all v3 HTML files for accessibility gaps in images and icons:
- img tags with empty or missing alt attributes
- Icon or emoji spans used as meaningful content without aria-label
- Link elements that are icon-only without accessible text

Apply WCAG AA compliant fixes. Use alt empty string for decorative elements. Add aria-label to meaningful icons. Report all findings before applying fixes.

title: Create v3/portfolio.html — featured work and case studies
body:
Create a NEW file v3/portfolio.html showcasing Offbeat, Inc. work and case studies.

Requirements:
- Match v3 dark futuristic design (same CSS variables, header/footer as index.html)
- Sections: Featured Projects grid (DatBotty autonomous system, Offbeat website v3, PrepDeck concept), Case Study highlights with results achieved
- Each project card: title, description, technology pills, outcome metric (e.g. '15 pages deployed autonomously')
- Include JSON-LD ItemList schema
- Add 'Portfolio' link to nav on ALL existing v3 pages
- Do NOT use 'proprietary' or 'unconventional' in any copy
- Buttons must NEVER lift or translate upward on hover
- Standard OG meta tags and meta description

title: Create v3/newsletter.html — Offbeat newsletter signup landing page
body:
Create a NEW file v3/newsletter.html for an Offbeat, Inc. newsletter signup.

Requirements:
- Match v3 dark futuristic design (same CSS variables as index.html)
- Headline: 'Stay ahead of the curve'
- Short description: what subscribers get (tech insights, DatBotty updates, AI/automation news)
- Email signup form that POSTs to a mailto: link (static-friendly, no backend needed)
- Privacy assurance line linking to privacy.html
- Add 'Newsletter' link to footer on all v3 pages (NOT main nav — keep nav clean)
- Include OG/meta tags
- Buttons must NEVER lift on hover
- Do NOT use 'proprietary' or 'unconventional'

title: Add social proof / metrics bar to v3/index.html
body:
Add a horizontal metrics/social-proof bar to v3/index.html between the hero and the features section.

Metrics to display in a row of 4 stat blocks:
- '15+ Pages' subtext 'deployed autonomously'
- '24/7' subtext 'always running'
- '93%' subtext 'task success rate'
- '$0' subtext 'AI model cost'

Styling: dark card row, numbers in large cyan font, subtext in muted white. No animation needed — static display only.
Do NOT use an animated counter (keep it simple and fast-loading).
Do NOT alter any existing content. Insert after the hero section.

title: Create v3/case-study-datbotty.html — DatBotty deep-dive case study
body:
Create a NEW file v3/case-study-datbotty.html — a detailed case study about building DatBotty.

Requirements:
- Match v3 dark design (same header/footer as index.html)
- Sections: The Challenge (24/7 autonomous ops), The Solution (GitHub Actions + Cloud Agent + OpenClaw), The Results (15 pages, 93% success, 0 cost), Key Technologies used
- Long-form narrative style, ~800 words total
- Include Article JSON-LD schema with datePublished: 2026-04-15
- No external images needed — use CSS-styled text and code blocks
- Add 'Case Study' link to footer of services.html and work.html only (not main nav)
- Standard OG/meta tags
- Do NOT use 'proprietary' or 'unconventional'

title: Add dark mode toggle to v3 pages
body:
Add a simple dark/light mode toggle to all v3 HTML pages.

Implementation:
- Small sun/moon icon toggle button in the top-right corner of the header
- Clicking toggles a 'light-mode' class on the <body> element
- Store preference in localStorage so it persists
- In light mode: swap --bg-deep to #f8f8fc, --bg-dark to #e8e8f0, text to dark, keep cyan accents
- Default is always dark (current design)
- Button styling: minimal icon-only, no border, no background, icon size 20px

Apply to ALL v3 HTML files. The toggle CSS and JS can be inline in each file.
BUTTONS must NOT lift or move on hover.
Report which files were updated.

title: Add PWA manifest and service worker stub to v3
body:
Add Progressive Web App support to the v3 website.

Files to create:
1. v3/manifest.json — PWA manifest with: name 'Offbeat Inc.', short_name 'Offbeat', start_url '/offbeat-website/v3/', display 'standalone', background_color '#020206', theme_color '#00d4ff', icons array pointing to a generic icon path
2. v3/sw.js — minimal service worker that caches the v3 HTML pages and CSS on install, serves from cache when offline

Files to update:
- Add <link rel='manifest' href='manifest.json'> to ALL v3 HTML files
- Add <meta name='theme-color' content='#00d4ff'> to all v3 HTML files
- Add minimal SW registration script to all v3 HTML files

Report what was created and which files were updated.

title: Add structured data: SiteNavigationElement schema to v3/index.html
Improve v3/services.html: add FAQ accordion section
body:
Add an inline FAQ section to v3/services.html.

5 questions to include:
1. How long does a typical project take?
2. Do you offer ongoing support after delivery?
3. Can you work with my existing tech stack?
4. What's your process for AI automation projects?
5. How do I get started?

Use the same accordion styling as v3/faq.html. Insert the section before the final CTA at the bottom of the page.
Do NOT modify any existing content. The FAQ link in the answer to question 5 should point to contact.html.
Buttons must NEVER lift on hover.

title: Add 'As featured in' logos section to v3/index.html
body:
Add a minimal 'Built With' / tech stack logos row to v3/index.html.

Show 6 technology logos/names as text badges only (no image files needed):
GitHub Copilot, Python, GitHub Actions, OpenClaw, Docker, SQLite

Styling: horizontal row of pill-shaped text badges, muted border color, small font, centered. Label above: 'Powered By' in small caps.
Insert between the hero stats bar and the features section.
Do NOT add this if a similar section already exists on the page.
Do NOT use actual external image URLs for logos — text-only badges.

title: Audit v3 pages for broken links and missing nav items
body:
Audit all v3 HTML files for link consistency.

Check:
1. Every nav link on every page points to a real file that exists in v3/
2. Footer links (privacy, contact, faq) are consistent across all pages
3. Any href='#' placeholder links that should have real destinations by now
4. Any links to files that no longer exist or moved
5. Missing nav items (e.g. if blog.html was added, is it in every nav?)

Report all findings as a numbered list with specific file + line references.
Fix any broken links or missing nav items directly.
Do NOT change any content — only fix nav and footer links.

title: Create v3/open-source.html — Offbeat open source contributions page
body:
Create a NEW file v3/open-source.html about Offbeat's relationship with open source.

Requirements:
- Match v3 dark futuristic design
- Section 1: Philosophy — how Offbeat uses and contributes to open source (OpenClaw, browser-use, etc.)
- Section 2: Tools We Use — grid of open source tools (Docker, Python, Playwright, SQLite, Node.js)
- Section 3: Community — brief note about building in the open
- Include OG/meta tags, JSON-LD WebPage schema
- Link from v3/about.html footer section
- Do NOT claim Offbeat releases its own open source software
- Do NOT use 'proprietary' or 'unconventional'
- Buttons must NEVER lift on hover

title: Add live GitHub stats badge to v3/status.html
body:
Add a GitHub repository activity section to v3/status.html.

Add a row showing:
- Link to GitHub repo (CrashyCrash/offbeat-website) with star/fork count if fetchable via public API
- Last commit date (from status-data.json if that field exists, or hardcoded fallback)
- Number of open issues labeled datbotty-task

Fetch data using fetch() in a small inline script that calls the public GitHub REST API (no auth needed for public repos).
Handle fetch failure gracefully — show 'Loading...' then 'unavailable' on error.
Insert the section near the top of the page under the existing metrics row.
Do NOT break any existing content.

title: Add hreflang and language meta to all v3 pages
body:
Add basic internationalization metadata to all v3 HTML files.

Add to every v3 page:
1. <html lang='en'> attribute (check if already present; add if missing)
2. <meta name='language' content='English'>
3. <link rel='alternate' hreflang='en' href='[full URL of this page]'>
4. <link rel='alternate' hreflang='x-default' href='https://crashycrash.github.io/offbeat-website/v3/'>

This is a search engine optimization best practice for establishing primary language.
Report which files were missing the lang attribute and which needed the hreflang links added.

title: Improve v3/blog.html with 2 additional blog post cards
body:
If v3/blog.html already exists: add 2 more blog post cards to the grid.

New posts to add:
1. Title: 'GitHub Copilot Cloud Agent: The Future of Autonomous Coding' — Date: April 2026, Tag: AI Automation, Excerpt: How the GitHub Copilot cloud agent autonomously creates pull requests from issue descriptions — and why this changes everything for small teams.
2. Title: 'DJ to Developer: Building an AI System That Actually Makes Money' — Date: April 2026, Tag: Indie Hacking, Excerpt: What I learned building DatBotty as a non-developer using AI to write most of the code.

If v3/blog.html does not exist: create it first with all existing cards from the original task plus these 2 new ones.
Match existing card styling exactly. Buttons must NEVER lift on hover.
